title: trema sampsonii
intro: Trema sampsonii, also known as Sampson's Trema, is a plant species in the family Cannabaceae, commonly found in tropical regions. Its leaves and stems are rich in medicinal properties, containing flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which are known for their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Trema sampsonii include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support immune function. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations, such as infusions and decoctions, to treat various ailments, and it is also used in folk medicine to make teas and tonics.
